content:
# Joshua Chapter 6 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 229-230 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_006_page_001.jpg`

---

**1** Now Jericho was shutting and shut up before the sons of Israel; none going out and none coming in.

**2** And YHWH said to Joshua, "See, I have given into your hand Jericho, and its king, and the mighty men of valor.

**3** And you shall go around the city, all the men of war, circling the city one time. Thus you shall do for six days.

**4** And seven priests shall carry seven rams' horn trumpets before the ark. And on the seventh day you shall go around the city seven times, and the priests shall blow on the trumpets.

**5** And it shall be, when they make a long blast with the horn of the ram, when you hear the sound of the trumpet, all the people shall shout with a great shout, and the wall of the city shall fall down in its place, and the people shall go up, each man straight before him."

**6** And Joshua son of Nun called the priests and said to them, "Take up the ark of the covenant, and let seven priests carry seven rams' horn trumpets before the ark of YHWH."

**7** And he said to the people, "Pass on and go around the city, and let the armed men pass on before the ark of YHWH."

**8** And it was, when Joshua had spoken to the people, that the seven priests carrying the seven rams' horn trumpets before YHWH passed on and blew on the trumpets, and the ark of the covenant of YHWH was going after them.

**9** And the armed men were going before the priests who blew the trumpets, and the rear guard was going after the ark, going on and blowing the trumpets.

**10** And Joshua commanded the people, saying, "You shall not shout, and you shall not let your voice be heard, and no word shall go out from your mouth, until the day I say to you, 'Shout!' Then you shall shout."

**11** And the ark of YHWH went around the city, circling it one time. And they came into the camp and lodged in the camp.

**12** And Joshua rose early in the morning, and the priests took up the ark of YHWH.

**13** And the seven priests carrying the seven rams' horn trumpets before the ark of YHWH were going on continually, and blowing on the trumpets. And the armed men were going before them, and the rear guard was going after the ark of YHWH, going on and blowing the trumpets.

**14** And they went around the city on the second day one time, and returned to the camp. Thus they did for six days.

**15** And it was on the seventh day, they rose early, at the ascending of the dawn, and they went around the city according to this manner seven times. Only on that day they went around the city seven times.

**16** And it was at the seventh time, the priests blew on the trumpets, and Joshua said to the people, "Shout! For YHWH has given you the city.

**17** And the city shall be devoted to destruction, it and all that is in it, to YHWH. Only Rahab the prostitute shall live, she and all who are with her in the house, because she hid the messengers whom we sent.

**18** And as for you, only keep yourselves from the devoted thing, lest you devote yourselves to destruction and take from the devoted thing, and make the camp of Israel devoted to destruction and bring trouble upon it.

**19** And all the silver and gold, and vessels of bronze and iron — it is holy to YHWH; it shall come into the treasury of YHWH."

**20** And the people shouted, and they blew the trumpets. And it was when the people heard the sound of the trumpet, the people shouted with a great shout, and the wall fell down in its place. And the people went up into the city, each man straight before him, and they captured the city.

**21** And they devoted to destruction all that was in the city, from man and unto woman, from young and unto old, and unto ox and sheep and donkey, with the edge of the sword.

**22** And to the two men who had spied out the land, Joshua said, "Go into the house of the woman, the prostitute, and bring out from there the woman, and all who belong to her, as you swore to her."

**23** And the young men, the spies, went in and brought out Rahab, and her father, and her mother, and her brothers, and all who belonged to her — and all her clans they brought out. And they set them outside the camp of Israel.

**24** And the city they burned with fire, and all that was in it. Only the silver and the gold, and the vessels of bronze and of iron, they gave into the treasury of the house of YHWH.

**25** And Rahab the prostitute, and the house of her father, and all who belonged to her, Joshua kept alive. And she has dwelt in the midst of Israel unto this day, because she hid the messengers whom Joshua sent to spy out Jericho.

**26** And Joshua swore an oath at that time, saying, "Cursed before YHWH is the man who rises up and builds this city, Jericho. With his firstborn he shall lay its foundation, and with his youngest he shall set up its gates."

**27** And YHWH was with Joshua, and his fame was in all the land.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Textual Basis
- **Primary source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 229-230, as photographed by the West Semitic Research Project (Bruce E. Zuckerman).
- **LXX consultation:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209, folio 166), Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana. The LXX preserves a shorter text in several places, particularly in vv. 3-5 and 15, reflecting possible haplography or a variant Vorlage.

### Key Translation Decisions

1. **v. 1 -- "shutting and shut up" (מַסְגֶּ֣רֶת וּמְסֻגָּרָ֑ה):** The Hebrew uses a participle + passive participle construction to emphasize the completeness of Jericho's closure. LOGOS preserves the double expression rather than smoothing to a single term.

2. **v. 2 -- "I have given" (נָתַ֣תִּי):** The Hebrew perfect (qatal) is used for a future action, the so-called "prophetic perfect," expressing divine certainty. LOGOS retains the perfect tense to reflect the Hebrew.

3. **v. 4 -- "rams' horn trumpets" (שׁוֹפְר֣וֹת הַיּוֹבְלִ֗ים):** Hebrew *shoferot hayovelim* -- literally "trumpets of rams/jubilees." The *yovel* is the ram (from which the horn comes) and also gives the Jubilee year its name. LOGOS renders "rams' horn trumpets" for clarity while retaining the semantic link.

4. **v. 5 -- "horn of the ram" (קֶ֣רֶן הַיּוֹבֵ֗ל):** Distinguished from *shofar* in this verse. The Hebrew text uses both *qeren* (horn) and *shofar* (trumpet) in the same verse, and LOGOS preserves both.

5. **v. 17 -- "devoted to destruction" (חֵ֥רֶם):** Hebrew *cherem* -- the ban of total consecration/destruction to YHWH. LOGOS uses "devoted to destruction" consistently to capture both the consecration and destruction aspects of this technical term. The LXX renders with anathema (ἀνάθεμα).

6. **v. 18 -- "lest you devote yourselves to destruction" (פֶּן־תַּחֲרִ֗ימוּ):** The wordplay on *cherem* is preserved: taking from the *cherem* makes the taker himself *cherem*. LOGOS maintains the root connection.

7. **v. 21 -- "from man and unto woman, from young and unto old":** The Hebrew merism pairs are preserved in their formal structure with repeated *min...we-'ad* ("from...and unto") rather than smoothing to "men and women, young and old."

8. **v. 25 -- "she has dwelt...unto this day":** The Hebrew uses the qal perfect of *yashav* followed by *'ad hayom hazeh*, the narrator's retrospective voice. LOGOS preserves the present-perfect aspect ("has dwelt") to capture the ongoing state at the time of writing.

9. **v. 26 -- "With his firstborn...with his youngest":** Hebrew *bikhoro...tse'iro* -- the price of rebuilding Jericho. This curse was fulfilled in 1 Kings 16:34 with Hiel the Bethelite. LOGOS preserves the preposition *be-* as "with," maintaining the ambiguity of whether this means "at the cost of" or "accompanied by."

### YHWH Occurrences
YHWH appears in vv. 2, 6, 7, 8 (x2), 11, 12, 13, 16, 17, 19 (x2), 24, 25, 26, 27 -- fifteen times in this chapter. LOGOS renders the Tetragrammaton consistently as YHWH throughout.

### LXX Variants of Note
- **v. 5:** The LXX lacks the detailed instruction about the long blast, presenting a more condensed command.
- **v. 15:** The LXX does not include the clarifying note "only on that day they went around the city seven times."
- **v. 19:** The LXX reads "holy" (ἅγιον) for the metals, aligning with the MT *qodesh*.
- **v. 26:** The LXX adds "in the youngest" (ἐν τῷ ἐλαχίστῳ) where the MT has *tse'iro*, both denoting the youngest son.
